I need to reset based on what our current environment is.' Arthur committed to benchmarks and a value-engineering sheet, and a POC for ANA and Spend Analysis was later called 'positive' per the rep's Next Step notes (undated within the Mar 2026 window), but no buyer-confirmed, John Deere-specific metrics validation is on record.

champion.narrative: Karen Pagorek has been the most consistent buyer-side contact since Dec 2025, driving the pricing walkthrough (18 Dec 2025), and the four solo connects that followed (28 Jan, 24 Feb, 31 Mar, 11 May 2026), and Next_Step__c names her (with Eduardo Viera) as the 'verified path to agreement.' However, there is no evidence she is actively advocating internally for Zycus (no described internal selling, no escalation attempts to the CFO), and she is the one who told Arthur the POC would not move forward while evaluating an alternative — behavior more consistent with a still-evaluating buyer than a secured champion. Status is developing, not confirmed.

competition.narrative: The SF Competitors__c field lists Oracle and SAP Ariba, but neither is corroborated by any call read in this sweep. The real, evidenced competitive picture is different: GEP is being actively countered via a planned spend-analysis pilot (Next_Step__c: 'Pilot Spend Analysis to counter GEP'); the do-nothing/internal-build alternative (TCS homegrown plus OpenAI/Ultrametrics partnerships) was described at length on 24 Jun 2025; and most urgently, on 11 May 2026 (~2 months ago) Karen Pagorek told Arthur she is doing a deep dive on 'another external I/O provider' she declined to name, and paused the POC as a direct result.

identify_pain.narrative: Karen Powers articulated clear, specific pain in the 24 Jun 2025 demo (~12.5 months ago): John Deere's homegrown TCS quoting tool and Excel-based bidding require heavy manual work, and customizing any purchased tool ('deorizing') was described as 'a nightmare.' She also named a specific cost-tracking gap (no clean bill-of-materials roll-up or cost-walk history in the current RFx/RFQ tool), repeated in the 18 Dec 2025 call. This pain is well evidenced and consistent across multiple calls.

economic_buyer.narrative: Joshua Jepsen (SVP & CFO) and Eduardo Viera (Global Director, Supply Management Indirect Materials & Services) are both listed as Executive Sponsor on the Opportunity Contact Role, which structurally confirms an economic buyer is identified. However, Joshua Jepsen has never appeared in any of the 13 discovered touchpoints, and Eduardo Viera's only direct 1:1 with Zycus was 30 Sep 2025 (~9.3 months ago), after which the Next Step notes explicitly flag needing 'a Jolt to action' on Eduardo. The stated path to agreement runs through Karen Pagorek and Eduardo Viera (per Next_Step__c, following the 24 Feb 2026 call, ~4.5 months ago), but neither economic buyer has been directly engaged recently.

decision_process.narrative: The process has moved through demo (24 Jun 2025), a workshop series (3 Nov and 17 Nov 2025), a detailed pricing/scoping call (18 Dec 2025), and a POC phase delivered 31 Mar 2026 (~3.3 months ago). But the POC was paused within roughly six weeks of delivery as John Deere re-assesses priorities (11 May 2026, ~2 months ago), and the buyer is evaluating an unnamed alternative I/O provider in parallel, meaning the process is no longer moving toward a defined decision date.

decision_criteria.narrative: In the 18 Dec 2025 call (~6.7 months ago), Karen Pagorek pressed for specific definitions ('I believe in the iSource section... can we define some of these terms a little bit more specifically') distinguishing iSource/e-auctions from agent-driven autonomous sourcing, and for clarity on the standalone iSaaS integration module pricing ($30k/yr). These criteria were being actively shaped through the pricing walkthrough and the subsequent three-prong pilot structure (Spend Analysis vs GEP, limited-scope iSource POC, ANA Tactical MRO pilot per Next_Step__c), but no finalized, buyer-confirmed criteria set exists.

Champion Karen Pagorek remains engaged but has no budget authority and stated POC 'will not move forward' on 2026-05-11. Deal should be Pipeline until EB re-engages and budget path is locked.

north_star_verdict.headline: POC paused since May 2026 while buyer explores internal I/O alternative; Eduardo Viera (Economic Buyer) silent for 9 months, no budget pathway confirmed, and close date now 229 days out despite multiple slips. Rep-initiated re-engagement scheduled for July 20th has yet to occur; outcome uncertain.

flags: Convert the 2026-07-20 call into a limited-scope proof-of-value pilot (30-day iSource RFX or ANA MRO negotiation) with a specific business outcome (e.g. 3-5% savings on MRO tail spend, or 2-week cycle-time reduction on a strategic sourcing event) — bypass the stalled business-case and benchmarking discussion by proving ROI in 30 days, faster than the internal I/O provider can deliver.

Identify and neutralize the 'eternal I/O provider' threat — Karen stated on 2026-05-11 they are deep-diving another provider (unnamed); Zycus must discover who it is (internal build? partner? competitor?) and position against it with speed-to-value and modular integration wedge on the 2026-07-20 call or the deal will be lost to that alternative.

Economic buyer Eduardo Viera has not engaged since approximately September 2025 (inferred from Next_Step 'need a Jolt to action'); no direct relationship, no budget approval pathway mapped (source: SF Next_Step__c multiple dates, Avoma call history 2025-06 through 2025-12 shows no Eduardo attendance).

CFO Joshua Jepsen, the named Executive Sponsor contact role, has never appeared on a single call in the deal's 13-month history; if the internal evaluation reaches budget sign-off, Zycus has no established relationship at that gate.

Single-thread risk: Karen Pagorek is sole active contact as of May-July 2026; all other stakeholders (Gia Duke, Zachary Wenzel, Karen Powers) last engaged December 2025 or earlier (source: Avoma call history, SF Next_Step__c).

Buyer paused POC in May 2026 and is 're-assessing priorities' while evaluating unidentified 'eternal I/O provider'; Next_Step explicitly names 'in-house build or competitive loss' as ramifications (source: SF Next_Step__c 2026-05-11).

Benchmarks and reference call promised January 2026 to validate 40% cycle-time reduction ROI claim remain undelivered as of POC pause May 2026, leaving value case unproven (source: Avoma call 2025-12-18, SF Next_Step__c).

hard.ais_why:
• On the 12/18/2025 call, champion Karen Pagorek pushed Arthur to define autonomous and agentic terms more precisely, specifically asking him to disentangle e-auctions, tactical spend, and autonomous sourcing with negotiation inside iSource. That terminology pressure is substantive AI dialogue from the buyer side, not surface-level interest.
• Karen then asked what a pilot might look like. Arthur proposed pilots covering iSource, autonomous sourcing, and spend analysis: a sandbox RFX event, an MRO category negotiation, and a 3-month spend cube prototype. Karen also pressure-tested ROI realism on the $48M savings claim, calling for transparency on the assumptions.
• The offsetting signal is that the three-prong AI POC was paused on 5/11/26. That is a real down-signal post-call. The buyer is still at "quantify incremental value" and remembers poor feedback from past supplier evaluations. Active autonomous-sourcing dialogue offsets the pause, but the pause is why this stays in the cautious band.
